title: Veggie Takoyaki
description: Takoyaki with mushroom instead of oyster

ingredients.name: Okonomi Sauce (also called 'Otafuku Okonomiyaki Sauce Kokusai')
Japanese Mayonaise (distinct flavour to western mayonnaise)

equipment.name: Takoyaki pan
equipment.note: A special tray-shaped pan for making Takoyaki. Has many small spherical holes.

equipment.name: Mixing bowl and whisk (or a big blender/mixer)

stages.label: Dashi & Mushrooms
stages.description: Prepare the Takoyaki by making the Dashi and fried mushrooms

steps.instruction: Place a {1}  of cold water into your saucepan and add {2} of Kombu
Bring to a gentle simmer, then remove the kombu
Add {1} kelp granules, and {2} of flax seed oil
Bring to the boil for 3 seconds, then turn off the heat
Allow the pot to stand for 10 minutes
Strain the Dashi, to remove the Kelp
Add {1} of butter to a frying pan, medium-high heat
Once butter is melted, add the chopped mushrooms
Sprinkle with 0.5tsp salt, 1tsp paprika, 1tsp gralic powder
Cook the mushrooms, stirring occasionally

stages.label: Batter
stages.description: Prepare for the Takoyaki by making the batter

steps.instruction: Crack 2 large eggs into your large mixing bowl
steps.hint: You can use a mixer/blender here if you have a big enough one

steps.instruction: Add and stir in the Dashi
Mix in the {1} of plain flour
Mix in {1} of your Japanese soy sauce and {2} of sea salt
Mix everything together, outcome should be quite watery and smooth

stages.label: Preparation - Takoyaki
stages.description: Prepare ingredients for cooking the Takoyaki

steps.instruction: Measure the {1} Tenkasu into a container
Measure the {1} Benishouga into another container
Peel spring onion, discard peel, finely slice and separate whites and greens
Ensure that all your fillings (Tenkasu, Benishouga, spring onion, cooked mushroom cubes) are arranged into easy to reach places

stages.label: Cooking - Takoyaki
stages.description: Cook the batter in our Takoyaki pan and add the fillings

steps.instruction: Place the Takoyaki pan over your hob and heat it until you can feel from a short distance that it is hot
Brush thoroughly with vegetable oil
Pour in the batter, completely filling the holes in the pan as well as the surrounds
Drop a cube of mushroom into each hole
Scatter whole pan liberally with Tenkasu, Benishouga, and spring onions
As the batter starts to firm, use your skewer to mark out a grid around each hole, lifting the batter that isn't in the holes
Insert the skewer into the base of each hole to roll it over and create a sphere
Remove the balls (toothpicks/skewers help here) and place on a serving tray/plate
Drizzle liberally with Okonomi sauce, Japanese Mayonnaise, and sprinkle with Aonori
